scenario: Vanta Health operates Camunda 8.8 Operate and has just fixed an orderValue variable on a stuck instance worth 240 dollars of telehealth co-pay. The operator now needs to actually re-execute the failing step so the incident clears, and wants to recall where in Operate the retry control is exposed for the affected instance.
question: Where does Operate expose the retry control that re-executes the failing step after the variable edit?

options.text: Operate exposes the retry control in two places on the process instance page: in the top right corner of the page and below Operations, either of which initiates a retry on the affected instance
Operate exposes a single retry control only inside the Incidents tab of the cluster console;  the per-instance page intentionally hides retry behind the cluster level operator role on the instance

Operate exposes a retry control only from the Tasklist application after the operator picks the corresponding user task;  process retries are gated through human-task completion in the documented flow

Operate does not expose a manual retry control at all on the per-instance page;  the engine automatically re-tries the failing step on a fixed schedule after any variable edit is saved on the instance

a.text: Correct. The docs say there are two places on the process instance page where the retry can be initiated: in the top right corner of the page and below Operations; either control restarts the failing step on the instance.

b.text: Incorrect. The retry control is exposed on the per-instance page in Operate rather than being moved out into a cluster-console Incidents tab gated by a cluster-level role.

c.text: Incorrect. Retry is initiated from the Operate process instance page, not from Tasklist, and the docs do not describe routing process retries through human-task completion.

d.text: Incorrect. The retry must be initiated manually from one of the two documented controls on the per-instance page, and there is no automatic re-try schedule triggered by a variable edit save.

explanation: Operate shows a retry control in two places on the process instance page: in the top right corner of the page and below the Operations section. Either control initiates a retry of the previously failing step after the variable has been corrected.
